rules: **NEVER** hardcode these social media platform API URLs — they change frequently, use environment variables
**NEVER** scrape these sites without rate limiting (max 1 request per 2 seconds)
**NEVER** store user credentials of these platforms — use OAuth or read-only public data
**NEVER** ignore robots.txt
**NEVER** crawl without delay (minimum 1 second between requests)
**ALWAYS** identify your scraper with a clear User-Agent
**NEVER** store crawled data without considering copyright/ToS
**NEVER** use unofficial WeChat APIs — they get blocked frequently
**ALWAYS** use official WeChat Open Platform endpoints
**NEVER** handle credit card numbers directly — use payment provider's hosted form
**NEVER** skip signature verification on payment webhooks
**ALWAYS** ask user before modifying any payment-related code
**NEVER** store passwords in plain text — always hash with bcrypt/argon2
**NEVER** put session tokens in localStorage — use httpOnly cookies
**NEVER** skip CSRF protection on state-changing endpoints
**NEVER** send user PII to LLM APIs without consent
**NEVER** trust LLM output without validation (use pydantic/zod schema)
**ALWAYS** add timeout and retry logic for LLM calls
**ALWAYS** log token usage to detect runaway costs
**NEVER** trust file extensions — validate MIME type and magic bytes
**ALWAYS** limit file size (default max 10MB)
**ALWAYS** scan uploads for malicious content before processing
